sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 0.5g. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 0.5g. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, this is exactly the kind of profile you want.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 27g. glycemic impact is a big part of the story. for legumes, the carb story matters less when fibre and protein are both backing it up.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 27g. glycemic impact is a big part of the story. for legumes, the carb story matters less when fibre and protein are both backing it up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 5.5g. essential amino acids score is where the section starts to wobble. legumes need protein support to feel worth the carbs.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 5.5g. essential amino acids score is where the section starts to wobble. legumes need protein support to feel worth the carbs.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin e at 1% DV, vitamin c at 3% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin e at 1% DV, vitamin c at 3% DV stand out most. that is not enough to move the verdict very far on its own.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: iron at 9% DV, magnesium at 8% DV, zinc at 6% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.
sections.subtitleText: iron at 9% DV, magnesium at 8% DV, zinc at 6% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side. cheap and convenient because very easy to use as a quick meal component. still keeps some fibre because not completely nutritionally empty despite the weaker category fit. has practical comfort-food appeal because people actually eat it, which matters in the real world.

displayItems.title: cheap and convenient
displayItems.explanation: Very easy to use as a quick meal component.

displayItems.title: still keeps some fibre
displayItems.explanation: Not completely nutritionally empty despite the weaker category fit.

displayItems.title: has practical comfort-food appeal
displayItems.explanation: People actually eat it, which matters in the real world.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side. cheap and convenient because very easy to use as a quick meal component. still keeps some fibre because not completely nutritionally empty despite the weaker category fit. has practical comfort-food appeal because people actually eat it, which matters in the real world.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next. added sugar is a major category problem because legumes are supposed to win on slow-carb behaviour, and this pushes the opposite way. processing drags the category fit down because the canned sweet-sauce format makes it a worse version of what legumes are supposed to be. weak protein usefulness for a legume because it does not deliver the strong fibre-plus-protein combo that defines elite legumes.

closing.summary: Baked Beans stands out for cheap and convenient and still keeps some fibre. but it also gets held back by added sugar is a major category problem and processing drags the category fit down. Best only when convenience outweighs the weaker fibre or protein payoff.
closing.useCaseNote: Best only when convenience outweighs the weaker fibre or protein payoff.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in D tier.
